page: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 44 vCPUs and 352 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor with an x86_64 architecture, it provides 22 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled running at a base speed of 2.7 GHz. The processor includes a 105 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for integer and bfloat16 operations. This instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark data indicates average multi-core CPU performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 36,646.73 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 15,596.0. Memory read bandwidth reaches 2,999.39 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 112.56 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. The platform demonstrates strong performance in specific workloads, including image processing and CPU-based encryption. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong, text generation for large 70B models is poor, yielding 2.41 tokens per second due to the lack of hardware GPU acceleration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3-highmem-44 offers high memory density per vCPU without the added cost of bundled local storage or GPU hardware. This makes it a viable option for memory-intensive applications that rely on network-attached storage. Tradeoffs include the complete absence of local NVMe drives and poor suitability for heavy LLM text generation. It is best suited for memory-bound workloads, database operations, software compilation, and medium-scale data processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server configured with 44 vCPUs and 352 GB of RAM, yielding an 8.0 GB RAM-to-vCPU ratio. It runs on an Intel Xeon 8481C x86_64 processor with 22 physical cores, a 2.7 GHz clock speed, and a 105 MB L3 cache. The hardware supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show average multi-core performance alongside strong capabilities in image processing, encryption, and small-model LLM prompt processing. However, text generation for large 70B models is poor. This instance is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy applications that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration, making it suitable for database operations, software compilation, and memory-bound enterprise workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server with 44 vCPUs, 352 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8481C processor. It lacks local storage and GPUs, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3-highmem-44 is a memory-optimized server with 44 vCPUs and 352 GB RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8481C processor.
tagline: A memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring high memory density and Intel AMX support for memory-bound enterprise workloads without local storage.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8481C processor with AVX-512 and AMX extensions
Strong performance in image processing and CPU encryption benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
Suited for database operations, software compilation, and memory-bound tasks
